user_prompt: Graphical abstract for the Netz quantitative engine technical report. Horizontal landscape flow diagram showing five stages left to right: (1) Data ingestion block labeled 'TimescaleDB: NAV returns, VIX, macro series' with small hypertable icon; (2) Regime identification block labeled 'Markov HMM on log-VIX + 9-signal composite' with two-state Markov chain visual; (3) Risk measurement block labeled 'Historical CVaR + Regime-conditional CVaR + GARCH(1,1)' with distribution tail shaded; (4) Portfolio construction block labeled '4-phase CLARABEL cascade + Black-Litterman posterior' with cascading arrows; (5) Output block labeled 'fund_risk_metrics 5367 rows, 95 to 100 percent coverage'. Connect stages with thick right-pointing arrows. Use a professional institutional color palette: dark navy blue for data, teal for regime, deep blue for risk, slate gray for optimization, forest green for output. Clean white background. Sans-serif typography. Minimal text, high contrast, suitable for cover of a technical report.

iterations.prompt:
Create a high-quality scientific diagram with these requirements:

VISUAL QUALITY:
- Clean white or light background (no textures or gradients)
- High contrast for readability and printing
- Professional, publication-ready appearance
- Sharp, clear lines and text
- Adequate spacing between elements to prevent crowding

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Clear, readable sans-serif fonts (Arial, Helvetica style)
- Minimum 10pt font size for all labels
- Consistent font sizes throughout
- All text horizontal or clearly readable
- No overlapping text

SCIENTIFIC STANDARDS:
- Accurate representation of concepts
- Clear labels for all components
- Include scale bars, legends, or axes where appropriate
- Use standard scientific notation and symbols
- Include units where applicable

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Colorblind-friendly color palette (use Okabe-Ito colors if using color)
- High contrast between elements
- Redundant encoding (shapes + colors, not just colors)
- Works well in grayscale

LAYOUT:
- Logical flow (left-to-right or top-to-bottom)
- Clear visual hierarchy
- Balanced composition
- Appropriate use of whitespace
- No clutter or unnecessary decorative elements

IMPORTANT - NO FIGURE NUMBERS:
- Do NOT include "Figure 1:", "Fig. 1", or any figure numbering in the image
- Do NOT add captions or titles like "Figure: ..." at the top or bottom
- Figure numbers and captions are added separately in the document/LaTeX
- The diagram should contain only the visual content itself


USER REQUEST: Graphical abstract for the Netz quantitative engine technical report. Horizontal landscape flow diagram showing five stages left to right: (1) Data ingestion block labeled 'TimescaleDB: NAV returns, VIX, macro series' with small hypertable icon; (2) Regime identification block labeled 'Markov HMM on log-VIX + 9-signal composite' with two-state Markov chain visual; (3) Risk measurement block labeled 'Historical CVaR + Regime-conditional CVaR + GARCH(1,1)' with distribution tail shaded; (4) Portfolio construction block labeled '4-phase CLARABEL cascade + Black-Litterman posterior' with cascading arrows; (5) Output block labeled 'fund_risk_metrics 5367 rows, 95 to 100 percent coverage'. Connect stages with thick right-pointing arrows. Use a professional institutional color palette: dark navy blue for data, teal for regime, deep blue for risk, slate gray for optimization, forest green for output. Clean white background. Sans-serif typography. Minimal text, high contrast, suitable for cover of a technical report.

Generate a publication-quality scientific diagram that meets all the guidelines above.
iterations.critique:
SCORE: 10

STRENGTHS:
- Perfect execution of the requested horizontal left-to-right flow with all five stages accurately represented.
- The requested color palette (navy, teal, deep blue, slate gray, forest green) is applied perfectly with high-contrast white text, ensuring excellent readability.
- Technical icons (hypertable, Markov chain, shaded distribution tail, cascading arrows) are cleanly integrated, enhancing scientific communication without feeling cluttered.

ISSUES:
- None. (The tiny artifacting on the left edge of the Stage 1 table icon is exceptionally minor and does not detract from the publication-ready quality).

VERDICT: ACCEPTABLE

early_stop_reason: Quality score 10.0 meets threshold 7.5 for report
